content.problem: Assumed need to migrate content between ~/.gemini/ and ~/.agent/ when both directories contain legitimate, evolving content
content.discovery: Both ~/.gemini/ (22 items) and ~/.agent/ (9 items) exist as real directories with substantial, legitimate content - this is not a migration scenario but evolution of the system
content.root_cause: System evolved to support both provider-specific configs (~/.gemini/) AND universal agent protocols (~/.agent/) simultaneously
content.lesson: Before attempting major structural changes, verify the actual problem vs. assumed problem. The system naturally evolved to have dual directories with different purposes, not fragmented copies that need merging.
incorrect_assumptions: Content needs migration from one to another

correct_understanding.system_evolution: Directory structure evolved organically to support both universal protocols and provider-specific configurations
correct_understanding.real_solution: Update documentation to accurately reflect current state and clarify use cases for each directory

improvements_needed: Update CROSS_COMPATIBILITY.md to document dual-directory architecture
Create bidirectional sync utilities if needed
Document specific use cases: ~/.agent/ for universal protocols, ~/.gemini/ for provider configs
Create verification script before making structural changes
preventative_measures: Always verify actual vs. assumed state with manual checks
Test structural changes on non-critical systems first
Preserve existing functional content instead of assuming migration needed
Document architectural decisions and their rationale clearly
